Cheap: only reads agentHashes mapping (no struct load). Use this for pure boolean checks, verifyAgentAndLog() for full flow.","params":{"agentId":"The agent being verified.","currentHash":"keccak256 of the agent config dict, computed right now in Python."},"returns":{"_0":"bool         true = agent is legit. false = tampered or revoked."}},"verifyAgentAndLog(string,bytes32)":{"details":"Same logic as verifyAgent() but NOT a view function so it can         emit IntegrityViolation when tampering is detected. This is what makes the judge's \"wow moment\".","params":{"agentId":"The agent to check.","currentHash":"keccak256 of the live config dict from Python."},"returns":{"result":"     VerificationResult struct \u2014 decoded by Web3.py."}}},"title":"AgentIdentityRegistry","version":1},"userdoc":{"kind":"user","methods":{"agentHashes(string)":{"notice":"agentId  \u2192  codeHash         Separate lightweight mapping for cheap hash-only reads.         Used by verifyAgent() so it doesn't load the full struct."},"agentRecords(string)":{"notice":"agentId  \u2192  AgentRecord         Primary lookup: given \"researcher\", return full record."},"getAgent(string)":{"notice":"Get full record for a given agentId."},"getAgentCount()":{"notice":"Returns how many distinct agents have been registered."},"getCodeHash(string)":{"notice":"Returns the stored codeHash for an agent."},"isRegistered(string)":{"notice":"agentId  \u2192  bool (has this agentId ever been registered?)         Needed because Solidity mappings return zero values for         non-existent keys \u2014 we must distinguish \"never registered\"         from \"registered with hash 0x000...\"."},"owner()":{"notice":"Contract owner \u2014 the wallet that deployed this contract.         Only owner can register or revoke agents."},"registerAgent(string,bytes32,string,string)":{"notice":"Register an agent with its identity fingerprint."},"registeredAgentIds(uint256)":{"notice":"List of all registered agentIds \u2014 for iteration in the UI."},"revokeAgent(string)":{"notice":"Revoke an agent \u2014 marks it inactive."},"verifyAgent(string,bytes32)":{"notice":"Fast boolean check \u2014 is this agent still the registered one?"},"verifyAgentAndLog(string,bytes32)":{"notice":"Full verification with event emission \u2014 use this in the main flow."},"verifyAgentFull(string,bytes32)":{"notice":"Full verification result \u2014 powers the \"Verify Integrity\" panel in UI."}},"notice":"Contract 3 of TrustChain \u2014 the \"silent substitution\" detector. PURPOSE ------- Prove that the agent which ran a task is exactly the agent that was registered at deploy time.  If anyone swaps the Researcher agent for a different model / prompt, the codeHash changes and verifyAgent() returns false \u2014 visible to the judge in real time. CODEHASH DECISION (from design doc \u2014 \"most critical decision\") -------------------------------------------------------------- We use keccak256 of the agent config dict because it captures:   - agent name / role   - model name  (e.g. \"gpt-4o\")   - model version   - system prompt (full text)   - temperature + any other config flags Both registerAgent() at startup AND the Verify button in the UI must compute the hash from this same config dict \u2014 never change the schema. REGISTRATION TIMING ------------------- Agents are registered ONCE via a deploy script before the demo starts (hardcoded approach \u2014 cleaner for hackathon).  FastAPI reads the four registered agentIds from .env and never re-registers unless you run the script again. VERIFY INTEGRITY BUTTON ----------------------- Checks BOTH:   1. The agent codeHash still matches what was registered on-chain   2. Re-derives the hash from the live config dict and compares Returns a structured result so the frontend can show a detailed breakdown.","version":1}},"settings":{"compilationTarget":{"src/AgentIdentityRegistry.sol":"AgentIdentityRegistry"},"evmVersion":"shanghai","libraries":{},"metadata":{"bytecodeHash":"ipfs"},"optimizer":{"enabled":false,"runs":200},"remappings":[":forge-std/=lib/forge-std/src/"]},"sources":{"src/AgentIdentityRegistry.sol":{"keccak256":"0x109b9bd304665a917fc7a96da4eaafa3945c19f6e8b44de1eb140e1f52e593ce","license":"MIT","urls":["bzz-raw://1fc5b74edd0acb2dd1a0340c12cf47c07bbee55413c6b3921f80c3a3b35d498a","dweb:/ipfs/QmbP96HLEpkyYYAJBRcmBTtd6tC6Hrr5ShMnkjtpTKKeqp"]}},"version":1}

constructor.details: Sets the deployer as owner.      Called once when you run: npx hardhat run scripts/deploy.js      msg.sender at deploy time = your wallet address from .env PRIVATE_KEY

getAgent(string).details: Used by the Trust Score panel in the frontend to show         registration metadata: who registered, when, which model.

getAgentCount().details: Used by the frontend to iterate registeredAgentIds[].

getCodeHash(string).details: Lightweight read — used by AgentAuditLog before calling verifyAgent().

returns._0: bytes32  Stored codeHash, or bytes32(0) if not registered.

registerAgent(string,bytes32,string,stri.details: Called ONCE per agent via the deploy script before the demo.         If the agent was already registered, this updates the codeHash         and emits AgentUpdated instead of AgentRegistered.

params.codeHash: keccak256 of the agent config dict (computed in Python)
params.modelName: e.g. "gpt-4o"   — for display only, not used in verification
params.modelVersion: e.g. "2024-11-20" — for display only Python side (Web3.py):   config = {"agentId": "researcher", "model": "gpt-4o", "version": "...", "prompt": "..."}   code_hash = Web3.keccak(text=json.dumps(config, sort_keys=True))   contract.functions.registerAgent("researcher", code_hash, "gpt-4o", "2024-11-20")           .build_transaction({...})

revokeAgent(string).details: After revoking, verifyAgent() returns false for this agentId.         Use this if an agent is compromised during the demo.         Revocation is permanent — you must re-register to re-activate.

verifyAgent(string,bytes32).details: Called by AgentAuditLog BEFORE every agent invocation in LangGraph.         Cheap: only reads agentHashes mapping (no struct load).         Also emits IntegrityViolation if the hash doesn't match —         Web3.py event listener catches this and alerts the frontend.         NOTE: view functions cannot emit events (events require state change).         So the event emission is in verifyAgentAndLog() below.         Use this for pure boolean checks, verifyAgentAndLog() for full flow.

params.currentHash: keccak256 of the agent config dict, computed right now in Python.

returns._0: bool         true = agent is legit. false = tampered or revoked.

verifyAgentAndLog(string,bytes32).details: Same logic as verifyAgent() but NOT a view function so it can         emit IntegrityViolation when tampering is detected.         Called by FastAPI's /verify endpoint (the "Verify Integrity" button).

verifyAgentFull(string,bytes32).details: Returns VerificationResult struct so the frontend can render         a detailed breakdown: which hash was stored, which was computed,         whether the agent is active, and whether they match.         This is what makes the judge's "wow moment".

params.currentHash: keccak256 of the live config dict from Python.

agentHashes(string).notice: agentId  →  codeHash         Separate lightweight mapping for cheap hash-only reads.         Used by verifyAgent() so it doesn't load the full struct.

agentRecords(string).notice: agentId  →  AgentRecord         Primary lookup: given "researcher", return full record.

getAgent(string).notice: Get full record for a given agentId.

getAgentCount().notice: Returns how many distinct agents have been registered.

getCodeHash(string).notice: Returns the stored codeHash for an agent.

isRegistered(string).notice: agentId  →  bool (has this agentId ever been registered?)         Needed because Solidity mappings return zero values for         non-existent keys — we must distinguish "never registered"         from "registered with hash 0x000...".

owner().notice: Contract owner — the wallet that deployed this contract.         Only owner can register or revoke agents.

registerAgent(string,bytes32,string,stri.notice: Register an agent with its identity fingerprint.

registeredAgentIds(uint256).notice: List of all registered agentIds — for iteration in the UI.

revokeAgent(string).notice: Revoke an agent — marks it inactive.

verifyAgent(string,bytes32).notice: Fast boolean check — is this agent still the registered one?

verifyAgentAndLog(string,bytes32).notice: Full verification with event emission — use this in the main flow.

verifyAgentFull(string,bytes32).notice: Full verification result — powers the "Verify Integrity" panel in UI.
